metadata.title: AI+1 — AI+1 series 1/4
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent; callout ink #161511 on cream. Do not retint.
metadata.greeting_note: English (HAI shortest-form budget) — HAI takes only the shortest forms (Hi · Ola · Hej · Ciao); never spell out HumanitariansAI in the greeting (the footer chip does that). Wagwan n/a (Bear only).
metadata.purpose: CUSTOMER pitch, not investor pitch: sell custom workshops, custom courses, and $1 Kindle books from a nonprofit doing good, and send viewers to the Humanitarians AI YouTube.
metadata.note: COLD OPEN LAW: B00 = ClaudeComposerAsk. SPARK-LINE LAW: every inner beat carries a short serif spark line. HANDOFF LAW: 2nd-to-last beat is the composer with a paste-ready action, greeting 'Your turn.'. OUTRO LAW: restate the title, poster serif, terracotta period. House grammar: >=1 PREDICT beat; verdict page carries the phase gate; decisions.json + DECISIONS.md ship beside this sheet.

beats.narration_text: … this is Liam, in for Bear. AI plus one — that's the name of Humanitarians AI's course line. In about ninety seconds here's the whole offer: a canonical version you can get today for roughly a dollar, a custom version built to your spec that you own the rights to transform, and all of it from a nonprofit where every purchase funds the mission.

The canonical layer first. Every course is a full Kindle textbook priced at one dollar — free if you have Kindle Unlimited — not a teaser with an upsell at the end. The complete video lectures are free on the Humanitarians AI YouTube. And every slide deck runs as a voiced, animated page on their site. That is the whole course, not a sample.

props.heading: The canonical layer first.
props.body: Every course is a full Kindle textbook priced at one dollar — free if you have Kindle Unlimited — not a teaser with an upsell at the end. The complete video lectures are free on the Humanitarians AI YouTube. And every slide deck runs as a voiced, animated page on their site. That is the whole course, not a sample.

beats.narration_text: When you need your own version — your program's examples, your institutional branding, your name on it — you email info at humanitarians dot AI. They quote you a price. What ships is a zip: the base videos, the exam banks, and the rights to transform the content into your book, your course, your workshop.

props.heading: When you need your own version — your program's examples, your institutional bra
props.body: They quote you a price. What ships is a zip: the base videos, the exam banks, and the rights to transform the content into your book, your course, your workshop.

beats.narration_text: The zip opens in any CLI — Claude Code, Codex, whatever ships next year. Point your tools at it, rename sections, reorder modules, rewrite examples for your field. You're not locked to their software, because there isn't any. The starting point is theirs; what you make of it is yours.

props.heading: The zip opens in any CLI — Claude Code, Codex, whatever ships next year.
props.body: Point your tools at it, rename sections, reorder modules, rewrite examples for your field. You're not locked to their software, because there isn't any. The starting point is theirs; what you make of it is yours.

beats.narration_text: Quick predict before the next claim. If you work inside a university learning management system — specifically Canvas — guess what the zip exports to. Think about the format and commit to a guess before the reveal.

props.heading: Quick predict before the next claim.
props.body: If you work inside a university learning management system — specifically Canvas — guess what the zip exports to. Think about the format and commit to a guess before the reveal.

beats.narration_text: Canvas. The zip exports a .imscc cartridge — Canvas's native import format. Upload it, confirm, and your course is standing with modules, videos, and exams in place. And if you'd rather have people run it for you: custom training from their instructors, or a white-label workshop your own experts can deliver.

props.heading: Canvas.
props.body: The zip exports a .imscc cartridge — Canvas's native import format. Upload it, confirm, and your course is standing with modules, videos, and exams in place. And if you'd rather have people run it for you: custom training from their instructors, or a white-label workshop your own experts can deliver.

beats.narration_text: One page summary. The canon: a dollar on Kindle, free video course on YouTube, voiced animated decks on site. The custom version: one email, one quote, a zip with base videos, exam banks, and transform rights. Opens in any CLI. Exports .imscc to Canvas. White-label workshops available. And every purchase funds a 501(c)(3) doing AI education work.

artifactLines: Canonical: $1 Kindle textbook (free on Kindle Unlimited) · free YouTube videos · voiced animated decks.
Custom: email info@humanitarians.ai for a quote — the zip ships base videos, exams, transform rights.
Yours: opens in any CLI · exports .imscc straight into Canvas · white-label workshops available.
From a 501(c)(3): purchases fund fellows, mentorship, and free AI education.
Phase gate: we build the base · you make it yours · your experts deliver it.

beats.narration_text: Your turn — this takes two minutes. The handoff prompt will have Claude find the Humanitarians AI course closest to what you teach, and surface its three canonical forms: the Kindle book, the YouTube playlist, and the voiced deck. Paste it now.

props.command: List the Humanitarians AI courses at humanitarians.ai/courses, find the one closest to what I teach, and show me its three canonical forms: the $1 Kindle book, the YouTube playlist, and the voiced deck on the site.

beats.narration_text: AI plus one. The details follow in this playlist — the canon, your version, and Canvas delivery. Every course is watchable free on this channel. Liam, in for Bear.

props.title: AI+1.
